focus: Gap B — curves and organic form for the Illustrator's Hand (piece #3): Bezier/path manipulation, organic/freeform shapes, controlled hand-drawn feel (roughen/jitter without sloppiness), smoothing, and corner softening as agent-callable SVG operations. Design-only, read-only.
prior_art: Wood, Isenberg, Isenberg, Dykes, Boukhelifa, Slingsby — 'Sketchy Rendering for Information Visualization' (IEEE TVCG 2012, 18(12):2749-2758). THE academic foundation: defines sketchy primitives by perturbing line/polygon/ellipse with a controllable 'degree of sketchiness'. OA PDF: https://openaccess.city.ac.uk/id/eprint/1274/1/wood_sketchy_2012.pdf. Citable basis for 'roughen without sloppiness' = sketchiness is a single bounded scalar, not free noise.

Rough.js (rough-stuff/rough, https://roughjs.com / https://github.com/rough-stuff/rough) — production implementation of Wood et al. Algorithm details (https://shihn.ca/posts/2020/roughjs-algorithms/): a LINE is roughened by jittering both endpoints inside a radius set by `roughness`, picking extra control points near the 50% and 75% marks, connecting with a Bezier for the 'bowing' curve; drawn TWICE (double-stroke); a length-dependent dampening step keeps long lines legible. ELLIPSE: sample n points (n scales with size), jitter each, deliberately leave the loop slightly open (last point joins points 2-3) so it reads hand-closed. FILL: rotate to hachure angle, scanline-intersect polygon, pair intersections; cross-hatch = two angles 90° apart; zigzag/dots are variants. PATHS normalized to Move/Line/Cubic then roughened. Params: `roughness`, `bowing`, `maxRandomnessOffset`, `seed` (seed => reproducible).

matplotlib `plt.xkcd()` / Jake VanderPlas 'XKCD-style plots' (https://jakevdp.github.io/blog/2012/10/07/xkcd-style-plots-in-matplotlib/, https://matplotlib.org/stable/api/_as_gen/matplotlib.pyplot.xkcd.html) — cleanest jitter recipe with concrete numbers: spline-resample the polyline (point count ∝ arclength), `coeffs = mag*N(0,0.01)`, low-pass FIR filter (`firwin(f1=50, f2=0.01*dist_tot, kaiser f3=15)`), then displace ALONG THE NORMAL: `x += response*dy/dist; y -= response*dx/dist`. Key insight: correlated/perpendicular FIR-smoothed jitter reads as a hand wobble; isotropic per-point noise reads as garbage. This is the 'without sloppiness' control.

Chaikin corner-cutting subdivision (1974) — smoothing/corner-softening: replace each point with two at 1/4 and 3/4 along each segment, iterate; converges to a quadratic B-spline. Refs: https://smarx.com/posts/2020/08/chaikin-curves-a-beautifully-simple-algorithm/, https://observablehq.com/@pamacha/chaikins-algorithm. Deterministic, parameter = iteration count. Inverse craft to roughening.

Fillet / tangent-arc corner rounding — given three vertices, emit line→circular-arc-tangent-to-both-edges→line for a clean radius-r softened corner (vs. Chaikin's global smoothing). Discussed against SVG arc 'A' command and rounded-rect rx/ry: https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/06/decoding-svg-path-element-curve-arc-commands/, https://svgwg.org/svg2-draft/shapes.html.

Perlin/simplex noise displacement of path points along polar/normal coordinates (generative-art practice) — continuous noise field gives organic freeform wobble with no abrupt jumps (neighboring points stay coherent). Refs: https://alexcodesart.com/drawing-noisy-circles-with-p5-js-a-deep-dive-into-polar-coordinates-and-perlin-noise/, https://www.gorillasun.de/blog/an-introduction-to-perlin-noise-in-p5js-and-processing/. Seedable alternative noise source to xkcd's FIR-filtered gaussian.

Inkscape Power-Stroke / SVG WG variable-stroke-width discussion (Schepers ISSUE-2271, http://www.schepers.cc/differentstrokes.html, https://lists.w3.org/Archives/Public/www-svg/2013May/0002.html) — CRITICAL constraint: SVG has NO native variable stroke-width. Tapered/calligraphic 'hand' line weight must be rendered as a FILLED OUTLINE path generated from a centerline + a width-vs-arclength profile (the Inkscape approach). Variable line weight is a fill-path-generation op, not a stroke attribute.
verified_libraries: svgpathtools (PyPI, https://github.com/mathandy/svgpathtools) — pure-Python Bezier/path engine: Path = sequence of Line/QuadraticBezier/CubicBezier/Arc; gives point(t), length(), tangent/normal vectors, curvature, poly1d conversion, continuous-subpath splitting. Core read/measure/rebuild substrate for every Hand curve op.
rough-py (https://github.com/cktlco/rough-py) — direct Python port of Rough.js. Verified implementation of seedable roughen-line / roughen-ellipse / hachure-fill; reuse as the reference algorithm.

matplotlib (`matplotlib.pyplot.xkcd`, `matplotlib.patheffects` sketch transform) — ships the FIR-filtered normal-jitter implementation with f1=50/f2=0.01/f3=15 defaults; usable as a verified jitter kernel and parameter baseline.
simplification (PyPI, urschrei/simplification, Rust-backed) — fast RDP + Visvalingam-Whyatt `simplify_coords`; point reduction before/after roughening (epsilon ~1.0, 0.01, 0.001).
rdp (PyPI, fhirschmann/rdp) — pure NumPy Ramer-Douglas-Peucker; lightweight alternative for the same simplification op.
shapely — `geometry.simplify()` (Douglas-Peucker) plus robust polygon/line predicates; useful for the native-SVG bbox/collision rewrite and hachure scanline polygon intersection.
noise / opensimplex (PyPI) — seedable Perlin/OpenSimplex fields for the noise-displacement variant of organic wobble (reproducible from seed).
deterministic_ops: chaikin_smooth(selector, iterations): corner-cutting subdivision; reproducible, no seed. Output geometry is a pure function of input + iteration count.
fillet_corners(selector, radius, min_angle): replace sharp polyline/path corners with tangent arcs (or short Beziers) of radius r. Deterministic; radius is the only knob.
resample_path(selector, points_per_unit_length): spline/arclength resample to uniform point density (prep shared by jitter and smooth). Deterministic.
simplify_path(selector, epsilon, algorithm=rdp|vw): RDP/Visvalingam point reduction to clean over-dense or post-roughen paths. Deterministic.

roughen_path(selector, roughness, bowing, seed): Rough.js/Wood-style endpoint+midpoint perturbation with a REQUIRED seed. Mechanically reproducible GIVEN the seed (seed frozen in decision log); the math itself is deterministic.
jitter_along_normal(selector, magnitude, correlation, seed): xkcd-style FIR/Perlin-smoothed displacement strictly along the path normal, required seed. Deterministic given seed.
double_stroke(selector, passes=2, jitter, seed): redraw a stroke 1-2 extra times with seeded micro-offset for hand-sketch density. Deterministic given seed.

stroke_to_variable_width_fill(selector, width_profile[(t,w)...]): convert a centerline stroke into a filled tapered outline path per Inkscape power-stroke (SVG lacks native variable width). Deterministic from the profile.
open_loop(selector, gap_fraction): nudge a closed ellipse/loop's endpoints apart by a small fraction so it reads hand-closed (Rough.js gap trick). Deterministic.
taste_ops: choose_sketchiness_level(target_region): pick the single bounded roughness/bowing/magnitude scalar AND which elements get organic treatment vs. stay crisp. The 'roughen WITHOUT sloppiness' judgment; only the critique eye can say a mechanism arrow stays crisp while a free-hand membrane outline breathes. Output: parameters + seed frozen into decision log.

select_organic_targets(scene): decide which strokes are structural truth (must stay exact: axes, leaders, mechanism arrows, panel frames) vs. aesthetic-freedom decorative/illustrative geometry eligible for roughen/jitter/variable-width. Taste- AND accuracy-gated.
tune_corner_softness(selector): choose Chaikin iterations / fillet radius so a shape reads 'hand-drawn confident' not 'wobbly amateur' or 'CAD-stiff'. Needs the eye.
balance_line_weight_rhythm(scene): design width profiles so weight varies with hierarchy (hero vs. supporting) rather than uniformly — directly attacks uniform_line_weight_monotony. Taste.

set_seed_aesthetically(selector): a seed is mechanical, but PICKING the seed whose particular wobble looks best is a taste call (try-N, let the eye choose); the chosen seed is then frozen = reproducible.
proposed_agent_api: hand.smooth(selector, iterations=2) -> Chaikin corner-cutting; DETERMINISTIC. Emits an aesthetic-freedom-layer path.
hand.soften_corners(selector, radius, min_angle_deg=160) -> tangent-arc fillet; DETERMINISTIC.
hand.roughen(selector, roughness=1.0, bowing=1.0, seed) -> Rough.js/Wood perturbation; seed REQUIRED and logged.
hand.jitter(selector, magnitude=1.0, correlation=0.85, source='fir'|'perlin', seed) -> normal-direction smoothed wobble (xkcd kernel); seed REQUIRED.
hand.double_stroke(selector, passes=2, jitter=0.5, seed) -> sketchy redraw density.
hand.taper(selector, width_profile=[(0,0.6),(0.5,1.4),(1,0.6)]) -> centerline->filled variable-width outline (Inkscape power-stroke); DETERMINISTIC.
hand.open_loop(selector, gap_fraction=0.03) -> hand-closed ellipse/loop look.

hand.resample(selector, density) + hand.simplify(selector, epsilon, algo='rdp') -> shared prep/cleanup. Contract reuses the existing recipe op = {id, class, selector, action{type,params}, rationale, semantic_guard{allowed, reason}, seed?}; new edit classes: organic_curve, corner_softening, hand_stroke_texture, variable_line_weight. ROUTING RULE: every hand.* op declares layer='aesthetic_freedom' and writes to an overlay/decoration layer drawn ON TOP of accuracy-locked nodes; the executor lifts the 10px/[0.5,2.0] nudge ceilings ONLY for that layer, while svg_semantic_diff still enforces text-identity/frame/element-inventory/color-by-id invariance on the locked layer. Each op is dry-run-planned, then validated through svg_semantic_diff before write.
critique_signal: "Drive the organic-curve ops off the existing aesthetic vocabulary, no new enum needed. PRIMARY trigger antipatterns (AESTHETIC_ANTIPATTERN_IDS): `childish_shape_language` (wobble overdone / reads cartoonish -> REDUCE roughness or revert) and `dead_flat_vector_finish` + `uniform_line_weight_monotony` + weak `handcrafted_finish` gate slot -> ADD organic treatment: roughen/jitter/variable-width. Lever dimensions `hand_craft` and `line_weight_rhythm` (AESTHETIC_LEVER_DIMENSIONS) are the direct knobs the eye reports on; their verdict (pass/weak/fail) and route (svg_polish vs. semantic_backport vs. human_art_direction) decide whether the Hand applies, tunes, or backs off. The critique loop runs before/after as the SVG_DELTA evaluation (improved | no_meaningful_change | regressed[overdecorated|semantic_drift] | needs_human_art_direction); a regressed->overdecorated verdict is the explicit 'too sketchy' brake that lowers the sketchiness scalar or reverts the seed. Per-edit, svg_semantic_diff stays the hard structural guard (text_identity_loss BLOCKER, frame_change/group_transform_risk MAJOR) so no organic op silently mutates accuracy-locked geometry."
amateur_failure_mode: "Adding ISOTROPIC per-point random noise to path coordinates (or naively jittering every anchor independently) — it produces a jagged, sloppy, 'corrupted-file' look, not a hand. The verified fix from xkcd/Rough.js/Wood: (1) resample to uniform density first, (2) displace ONLY along the path normal, (3) low-pass FIR- or Perlin-smooth the noise so it is correlated (neighboring points move together = a smooth wobble), (4) keep sketchiness a single bounded, SEEDED scalar. Two more traps: (a) applying organic treatment to structural-truth geometry (mechanism arrows, axes, leaders, panel frames) — these must stay crisp and accuracy-locked; roughening them is ugly AND scientifically wrong; (b) faking variable line weight by tweaking the stroke-width attribute — SVG has no native variable width, so a true tapered hand line must be generated as a filled outline path from a centerline + width profile. Skipping the seed (non-reproducible output) also breaks the version-controlled-SVG reproducibility contract."

focus: Gap A depth/shading sub-engine of the Illustrators Hand (piece 3): gradients, soft shadows, material-volume on flat schematics, as an aesthetic-freedom layer ON TOP of accuracy-locked nodes. Tension: svg_semantic_diff.py lists filter/mask/clipPath as UNSUPPORTED_TAGS (BLOCKER), forbidding the filters that make soft shadows. Resolution: filter-FREE gradient + grounding geometry by default (passes guard), blur filters gated behind a widened guard on the aesthetic layer only. Read-only design.
prior_art: Lambert cosine diffuse model (intensity proportional to cosine of angle between light vector and surface normal; terminator, core shadow, reflected light) is the deterministic basis for placing gradient stops on cylinders and spheres. https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/lambertian-model

Five-value illustrator shading of primitive forms (highlight, light, mid, core shadow, reflected light) for cylinder/sphere/cone is the manual convention gradient stops emulate. https://www.toadhollowstudio.com/wp_blog/2016/04/beginners-shade-a-pyramid-half-sphere-and-cylinder/

Cel or flat-tone shading uses two or three flat hard-edged bands instead of a continuous gradient and is filter-free, print-safe, vector-exact. https://www.udit.es/en/cel-shading-que-es-su-proceso-paso-a-paso-y-10-ejemplos-imprescindibles/

SVG soft-shadow primitive chain (SourceAlpha, feGaussianBlur stdDeviation, feOffset dx dy, feFlood/feComponentTransfer opacity, feMerge) and the feDropShadow shorthand. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Element/feGaussianBlur

feSpecularLighting and feDiffuseLighting (surfaceScale, specularExponent) are SVG-native lighting filters that synthesize highlight and diffuse from an alpha bump. https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/filters.html
Flat-design long shadow and contact or grounding shadow (a soft ellipse hugging an object base to stop it floating) give pure-geometry editorial depth. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ambient_occlusion

SVG gradient mesh was removed from SVG 2.0, is Inkscape-only with no browser support and is a reproducibility trap; approximate with stacked radialGradients. https://librearts.org/2018/05/gradient-meshes-and-hatching-to-be-removed-from-svg-2-0/

color-interpolation-filters defaults differ across browsers (Safari sRGB versus Chrome and Firefox linearRGB) so feGaussianBlur is non-deterministic unless set explicitly. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Attribute/color-interpolation-filters

Print-fidelity caveat: vector editors rasterize filter shadows on PDF export at about 200 DPI, breaking the SVG-as-truth vector-exact promise. https://community.adobe.com/t5/indesign/indesign-pdf-export-drop-shadow-effects-are-200-dpi-low-res/m-p/10097557
verified_libraries: svgwrite (PyPI, svgwrite.readthedocs.io): pure-Python SVG authoring with first-class LinearGradient/RadialGradient classes, defs, stop-color, filter-effect support; the builder for emitting gradient/shadow defs and patching fills

svgpathtools (PyPI/GitHub mathandy/svgpathtools, conda-forge): read/write/transform SVG path as Line/Quadratic/CubicBezier/Arc/Path; computes geometric bbox/centroid/base-edge for grounding shadows WITHOUT pdftotext, replacing the dead TikZ/PDF bbox path of check_collisions.py

svgelements (PyPI): first-class .fill/.stroke/.stroke_width and transform/color parsing; supports the per-id optical-signature inventory the Hand must preserve and resolving effective fill before recoloring

lxml: namespaced XML round-trip for inserting defs gradient/filter nodes and rewriting fill attributes with stable serialization; xml.etree is the in-repo parser in svg_semantic_diff.py, lxml is the attribute-order-stable upgrade
deterministic_ops: build_axial_gradient(node_bbox, light_vec, value_ramp): place 5 Lambert value stops from a fixed light vector; same input yields byte-identical defs
build_radial_sphere_gradient(cx, cy, r, light_vec): radialGradient with fx/fy offset toward light per cos law (highlight to light to mid to core to reflected); pure math
build_cylinder_gradient(axis, width, light_vec): linearGradient perpendicular to the axis with symmetric core-shadow stops
make_contact_shadow_ellipse(base_bbox): flattened ellipse hugging the node base, radialGradient fading to 0 opacity; FILTER-FREE grounding shadow, geometry only
make_long_shadow(node_outline, angle=45, length, fade_to=bg): extruded gradient path fading to background; computed offset/fade
make_bevel_edge(node_outline, light_vec, depth): lit-edge highlight stroke plus dark-edge shadow stroke; fake bevel, no filters
quantize_gradient_to_cel(gradient, bands=3): replace smooth stops with hard-edged equal-offset stops; mechanical cel/flat-tone
register_light_rig(light_vec, key, ambient): one document-level light constant every op reads, guaranteeing coherent light across panels
set_color_interpolation_filters sRGB on every emitted filter: mechanical hardening so blur is browser-deterministic
compute_geometric_bbox(node) via svgpathtools: native bbox/centroid/base-edge replacing the dead pdftotext -bbox path; prerequisite for every placement
taste_ops: choose_light_direction(scene): WHICH way the key light points (e.g. top-left 135deg) is aesthetic; once chosen it freezes into register_light_rig and becomes deterministic. Signal: cross_panel_grammar / dead_flat_vector_finish

decide_which_nodes_get_volume: a flat schematic should NOT shade everything; over-shading reads childish_shape_language / poster_gradient_decoration. Editorial judgment (visual_hierarchy, weak_hero_anchor)
tune_shadow_softness_and_opacity: stdDeviation/dy/alpha that reads subtle-grounded vs cartoon is taste; the op accepts the value, taste picks it (handcrafted_finish vs overdecorated_or_cartoonish)
pick_gradient_vs_cel: smooth Lambert ramp vs 2-band cel is a maturity/journal-fit call (maturity_restraint, journal_fit)
set_value_contrast_range: core-shadow darkness / highlight brightness (ramp amplitude) governs restrained-editorial vs plastic; taste (maturity)

approve_filter_use_at_all: because blur rasterizes on PDF export and is guard-flagged, electing a real feGaussianBlur vs the filter-free ellipse needs human-art-direction sign-off (print_scale_finish, semantic_preservation)
proposed_agent_api: hand.add_volume_shading(selector, form=sphere|cylinder|plane, light=@doc_light): emits a linear/radialGradient into defs and rewrites ONLY the target node fill to url(grad); never touches geometry/text/ids/stroke topology; routes through svg_semantic_diff before write
hand.add_contact_shadow(selector, softness, opacity): grounding ellipse plus radialGradient as a NEW aesthetic-layer g.fa-decor BEHIND the node; filter-free so it passes the UNSUPPORTED_TAGS guard
hand.add_long_shadow(selector, angle, length): extruded gradient path in the decor layer; geometry-only
hand.add_bevel(selector, light=@doc_light, depth): lit/dark offset edge strokes in the decor layer
hand.cel_quantize(selector, bands): convert an existing shading gradient to hard-stop bands; print-safe, vector-exact
hand.set_document_light(vector, key, ambient): write/lock the single doc-level light every shading op reads (cross-panel coherence)

hand.add_soft_shadow_filter(selector, stdDeviation, dx, dy, opacity, color_interpolation=sRGB): the ONLY filter-emitting op; explicit opt-in, auto-sets color-interpolation-filters=sRGB, hard-gated behind needs_human_art_direction because filters rasterize on PDF export

Shared contract: every op reuses the svg_polish_recipe.py shape selector/action/rationale/semantic_guard(allowed,reason) with dry-run plan plus bounded execution; accuracy-lock invariants (text identity, frame, per-id color, structural inventory) checked by svg_semantic_diff, while decor-layer nodes are exempted via an aesthetic_layer allowlist so added defs and g.fa-decor do not trip element_inventory_change/unsupported_svg_feature
critique_signal: Drive gap-A ops off critique_schema_vocab.py: trigger shading when AESTHETIC_ANTIPATTERN_IDS has dead_flat_vector_finish or uniform_line_weight_monotony, or when the handcrafted_finish AESTHETIC_GATE_SLOT verdict is weak/missing; lever dimensions hand_craft and component_fidelity request volume, color_harmony bounds the gradient palette to locked style tokens. Restraint is policed by the opposite signals: overdecorated_or_cartoonish, poster_gradient_decoration, childish_shape_language, decorative_detail_without_explanatory_value MUST gate every shading op (block if the depth carries no explanatory value). Light coherence is judged by cross_panel_grammar / panel_style_mismatch. Routing on EDITORIAL_POLISH_PATHS and SVG_DELTA_ROUTES: a filter-free shading edit routes accept_svg_polish; any op that introduces a real filter or changes structural inventory routes semantic_backport_required; electing blur despite the PDF-rasterization caveat routes needs_human_art_direction. Regression watch via SVG_DELTA_REGRESSION_CATEGORIES: overdecorated, print_scale_regression, journal_mismatch. The dead enum members tikz_patch/continue_tikz are not emitted by the Hand.
amateur_failure_mode: Shading everything uniformly and reaching straight for blur drop-shadows: (1) blanket gradients on every box/arrow produce poster_gradient_decoration plus dead_flat_vector_finish, so the schematic looks like a generic SaaS slide, not a journal figure; (2) inconsistent light because no document light was registered, tripping cross_panel_grammar; (3) using feGaussianBlur/feDropShadow as the default soft-shadow tool, which (a) is flagged unsupported_svg_feature by svg_semantic_diff, (b) rasterizes to about 200 DPI on PDF export destroying the vector-exact SVG-as-truth promise, and (c) renders differently across browsers because color-interpolation-filters defaults diverge (Safari sRGB vs Chrome/Firefox linearRGB); (4) cranking value contrast so forms look plastic/cartoonish (overdecorated_or_cartoonish); (5) adding depth with no explanatory meaning (decorative_detail_without_explanatory_value), or worst, letting a gradient or shadow overlap or recolor an accuracy-locked node and silently change what the schematic asserts -- the structural guard exists to block exactly that. The disciplined path: filter-free grounding ellipses plus Lambert gradient stops on only the hero forms, one locked light vector, cel-quantized when journal restraint demands it, with real blur reserved for an explicit human-approved exception.

focus: Scientific color systems for the Illustrator's Hand (piece #3): assigning, generating, and verifying SEMANTIC-ROLE color on SVG-native schematics (gap A, aesthetic finish). Scope is schematic role-color (sulfur=amber, charge=blue, hidden-edge=gray-dashed), NOT data colormaps (Graph Hub owns those). Core principle uncovered: color work splits cleanly into a DETERMINISTIC half (palette lookup, CVD/contrast/deltaE verification, perceptual lightness/chroma math in OKLCH/CAM02-UCS — all reproducible and unit-testable) and a TASTE half (which role gets which hue, harmony mood, hero emphasis, restraint — needs a critique signal). The Hand should hard-enforce the deterministic half as part of the accuracy/structure lock (palette-token discipline = an extension of svg_semantic_diff's color-by-id invariant), and route the taste half through the existing critique vocabulary (color_harmony lever, accessibility_color_robustness audit, semantic_color_remap finding, dead_flat_vector_finish antipattern). Existing Style Lock to port: Paul Tol Muted hex set (cAmber #997A1E, cBlue #4477AA, cRed #CC6677, cTeal #44AA99, cGray rgb70) with frozen role bindings — survives as SVG color tokens; the .sty LaTeX mechanism dies.
prior_art: Paul Tol Colour Schemes (SRON Technical Note SRON/EPS/TN/09-002 issue 3.2, 2021): bright/high-contrast/vibrant/muted qualitative sets are colorblind-safe; muted is the current figure-agent Style Lock palette. https://personal.sron.nl/~pault/ and https://packages.tesselle.org/khroma/articles/tol.html

Okabe & Ito (2008) 8-color CVD-safe qualitative palette: #E69F00 #56B4E9 #009E73 #F0E442 #0072B2 #D55E00 #CC79A7 #000000; spans a wide luminance range so it survives grayscale + CVD collapse. https://easystats.github.io/see/reference/scale_color_okabeito.html

Fabio Crameri Scientific colour maps (v8.0.1) — perceptually uniform, CVD-safe, grayscale-safe; ~60 maps incl. categorical (S-suffix) and qualitative (roma/batlow); the rigorous source for any ordered/role-gradient need on a schematic (depth/charge shading), not data plots. https://www.fabiocrameri.ch/colourmaps/

Bjorn Ottosson OKLab/OKLCH (2020): perceptually-uniform cylindrical space; equal numeric step in L/C/H = equal perceived step, fixing HSL's hue/lightness non-uniformity (esp. blue/purple). The right space for mechanical lighten/darken/desaturate ops. https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2024/10/interview-bjorn-ottosson-creator-oklab-color-space/

Machado, Oliveira & Fernandes (2009) physiologically-based CVD model — the standard for anomalous-trichromacy severity simulation; Vienot 1999 / Brettel 1997 for dichromat extremes. Drives the CVD-distinctness verification op. https://www.inf.ufrgs.br/~oliveira/pubs_files/CVD_Simulation/Machado_Oliveira_Fernandes_CVD_Vis2009_final.pdf

APCA (Advanced Perceptual Contrast Algorithm, WCAG 3 draft): unlike WCAG 2's flat ratio, accounts for stroke thickness + spatial frequency — directly relevant to thin schematic strokes/leader lines; Lc 15 ~ invisibility floor for thin lines. https://git.apcacontrast.com/documentation/APCAeasyIntro.html

WCAG 2.1 non-text contrast: 3:1 minimum for graphical objects/state indicators — the conservative floor for label-vs-backdrop and stroke-vs-canvas. https://horizon.servicenow.com/guidelines/accessibility/color-contrast

Color-harmony geometry (analogous 30 deg, complementary 180, triadic 120, split-complement, tetradic) as hue-angle operations on a wheel — the deterministic generator behind a taste-chosen scheme. https://colorpickerimage.org/color-harmony/

Semantic / functional color theory: color assigned by ROLE with consistent meaning + redundant (non-hue) coding for CVD, distinct from decorative or data-magnitude color. https://snapied.com/glossary/semantic-color-application

Existing figure-agent Style Lock: /Users/choemun-yeong/workspace/ResearchOS/[figure-agent]/plugins/figure-agent/styles/polymer-paper-preamble.sty — Paul Tol Muted definecolor set + frozen role bindings (printatom S->cAmber else cGray; iso charge->cBlue; iso hidden edge->cGray dashed; trapShallow->cAmber, trapDeep->cBlue!45!cRed). This file IS the color contract to port to SVG tokens.
verified_libraries: ColorAide (Python, pip install coloraide; facelessuser.github.io/coloraide) — PRIMARY agent color engine. Verified: OKLab/OKLCH native + CSS oklch() output; CVD filters protan/deutan/tritan via Brettel-1997/Vienot-1999/Machado-2009 incl. anomalous severity; deltaE methods 76/cmc/94/2000/hyab/ok/itp/jz/99o/cam16/hct; color harmonies (analogous/complementary/triadic/split/tetradic); contrast (WCAG) + Y()/luminance + gamut mapping. Single dependency covers assign, verify, and manipulate.

cmcrameri (Python, pip install cmcrameri, also conda-forge; wraps Crameri Scientific colour maps v8.0) — VERIFIED for perceptually-uniform + CVD-safe + grayscale-safe ordered/categorical color when a schematic needs a role GRADIENT (depth/charge intensity) or a >8-role categorical set. Categorical maps carry S suffix; .resampled(n) discretizes.

colorspacious (Python, pip install colorspacious; colorspacious.readthedocs.io) — VERIFIED CAM02-UCS/LCD/SCD perceptual space + deltaE() defaulting to CAM02-UCS; use when CAM02-UCS distinctness is wanted as the authoritative perceptual-difference metric (Luo et al. 2006) over OKLab deltaE.

colour (colour-science, Python, pip install colour-science; github.com/colour-science/colour) — VERIFIED heavyweight reference impl of CAM02-UCS/CAM16-UCS deltaE and full colorimetry; reserve for cross-checking the lightweight ColorAide path, not first-line (heavier dependency).
khroma (R, CRAN) — VERIFIED canonical Paul Tol + Okabe-Ito implementation with built-in CVD diagnostics; reference/oracle for the frozen token hex values, not the Python runtime path.
deterministic_ops: palette_token_resolve(role) -> hex: look up a role in the frozen SVG color-token table (ported Paul Tol Muted: sulfur->#997A1E, charge->#4477AA, deep-trap->blend, hidden-edge->cGray, etc.). Pure map; no creativity. This is the SVG successor to the .sty definecolor block.

oklch_adjust(hex, dL, dC, dH) -> hex: mechanical lighten/darken/desaturate/hue-rotate in OKLCH via ColorAide so equal numeric step = equal perceived step (e.g. derive a fill from a stroke by fixed - delta L). Reproducible given inputs.

cvd_distinctness_check(role_colors[], type in {protan,deutan,tritan}) -> pass/fail + min pairwise deltaE: simulate via ColorAide protan/deutan/tritan (Machado/Vienot) then deltaE 2000/ok; flag any pair below threshold. Deterministic gate.

grayscale_separation_check(role_colors[]) -> pass/fail: convert to OKLab L / relative luminance; flag any two roles whose lightness differ by < threshold (redundant-channel guarantee for B/W print + CVD collapse). Deterministic.

non_text_contrast_check(stroke_or_label_hex, backdrop_hex, stroke_width) -> WCAG ratio + APCA Lc: enforce >=3:1 (WCAG non-text) and APCA Lc floor scaled by stroke width (thin lines need higher Lc; Lc15 ~ invisibility). Deterministic given geometry.

harmony_generate(anchor_hex, scheme) -> hex[]: apply fixed hue-angle offsets (analogous 30, complementary 180, triadic 120, split, tetradic) in OKLCH; mechanical given anchor+scheme. (WHICH scheme is taste; the generation is deterministic.)
gamut_clip_to_srgb(hex) -> hex: ColorAide gamut-map any OKLCH-authored color back into sRGB so the SVG renders identically everywhere. Deterministic.

palette_invariance_diff(source_svg, edited_svg) -> findings: extend svg_semantic_diff color-by-id; any fill/stroke not resolving to a registered token, or any change to an accuracy-locked node's color, raises semantic_color_remap. Deterministic structural guard.
taste_ops: assign_role_to_hue(role, candidate_tokens): WHICH semantic role gets WHICH palette hue (is 'oxidized state' red or amber?). Convention-driven, figure-dependent — needs the eye. Constrained by determinism (must come from the registered token set + pass CVD/contrast gates) but the choice itself is judgment.
choose_harmony_scheme(figure_intent): analogous (calm/related mechanism) vs complementary (contrast two opposing states) vs muted-monochrome (restraint). Mood call routed via color_harmony lever.
set_hero_color_emphasis: which single element earns the one saturated accent against an otherwise desaturated field (fights weak_hero_anchor / dead_flat_vector_finish). Pure compositional taste.

tune_restraint_vs_decoration: how many distinct hues before the schematic looks cartoonish (childish_shape_language / poster_gradient_decoration / overdecorated). Deterministic check can count hues; the threshold of 'too much' is taste.
apply_depth_gradient_subtlety: when a role gradient (Crameri map) reads as scientific depth-cue vs as decorative poster-gradient. Taste verdict on subtlety.

cross_panel_color_grammar: keeping role->hue bindings identical across a multi-panel figure so blue always means the same thing (cross_panel_semantic_grammar). Consistency is mechanical to check, but the chosen grammar is an art-direction decision.
proposed_agent_api: resolve_token(role) -> hex  # frozen SVG color-token table; deterministic, accuracy-locked
derive_shade(hex, delta_lightness=, delta_chroma=, delta_hue=) -> hex  # OKLCH via ColorAide; deterministic

set_fill(element_id, token_or_hex) / set_stroke(element_id, token_or_hex)  # widened polish action set; on accuracy-locked nodes only token-resolved colors allowed, on aesthetic-freedom layer any in-gamut color allowed
apply_role_gradient(element_id, crameri_map, stops=n)  # SVG linearGradient from a CVD-safe Crameri categorical/sequential map; aesthetic-layer only
generate_harmony(anchor_hex, scheme) -> hex[]  # OKLCH hue-angle offsets; deterministic generation
verify_cvd(role_colors[], types=[protan,deutan,tritan]) -> report  # ColorAide simulate + deltaE; gate
verify_grayscale(role_colors[]) -> report  # OKLab lightness separation; gate
verify_contrast(fg_hex, bg_hex, stroke_width=) -> {wcag, apca_lc}  # non-text contrast gate
audit_palette_invariance(source_svg, edited_svg) -> findings  # extends svg_semantic_diff; raises semantic_color_remap on unregistered/locked-node color change
critique_signal:
"Reuse the EXISTING figure-agent critique vocabulary verbatim — color taste-ops route through these, deterministic ops gate before write. Drivers: AESTHETIC_LEVER_DIMENSIONS.color_harmony (the primary color taste lever; verdict pass/weak/fail/needs_human) and the implied line_weight_rhythm pairing for stroke contrast. Antipatterns that FIRE the color taste-ops: dead_flat_vector_finish + uniform_line_weight_monotony (gap A: palette too flat/lifeless -> apply derive_shade hero emphasis), childish_shape_language / poster_gradient_decoration / overdecorated_or_cartoonish (too many hues / decorative gradient -> tune restraint), weak_hero_anchor (no color hero -> set_hero_color_emphasis). Top-tier audit key accessibility_color_robustness (verdict pass/weak/fail/needs_human) is the home for verify_cvd + verify_grayscale + verify_contrast results. WEAKEST_PANEL_WEAKNESS_TYPES.color and cross_panel_semantic_grammar drive cross_panel_color_grammar. The structural guard finding semantic_color_remap (from svg_semantic_diff) is the BLOCKER that fires when a color edit touches an accuracy-locked node or drops a registered token. AESTHETIC_GATE_ROUTES: svg_polish for token-safe taste tweaks, semantic_backport for remap violations, needs_human_art_direction when color_harmony verdict = needs_human. Two-layer contract: deterministic gates (CVD/grayscale/contrast/token-invariance) MUST pass before any write; color_harmony/hero/restraint verdicts only re-route an already-valid edit."
critique_signal="(see above)"
amateur_failure_mode: "The amateur picks colors by raw RGB/HSL eyeballing in the wrong space: HSL lighten makes blues and yellows shift unequally (non-uniform), so 'the same' tint looks fine on one role and muddy on another. They choose hues that look distinct ON THEIR SCREEN but collapse under deuteranopia (red/green schematic states become identical) and vanish in grayscale print because the roles share lightness — no redundant channel. They over-saturate everything (every element a vivid hue) producing the childish/poster look instead of one restrained palette with a single hero accent (dead_flat_vector_finish is the opposite failure: a timid all-gray figure with no hero at all). They let a decorative gradient masquerade as meaning, or recolor a structurally-locked element so blue stops meaning the same thing across panels (semantic_color_remap, cross-panel grammar break). They verify contrast with a flat WCAG ratio that ignores stroke width, so hairline leader lines pass the number but are physically invisible (APCA Lc < 15). Root cause: treating color as a per-element aesthetic whim instead of a frozen role->token contract verified by deterministic CVD/grayscale/contrast math — exactly the deterministic-vs-taste split the Hand enforces."

focus: Labels & typography with scientific precision for the Illustrator's Hand (piece #3): deterministic text placement, leader-line routing, math/units rendering, alignment, legibility, and label-collision avoidance — split into mechanical (reproducible) ops vs. taste (critique-driven) ops, layered on top of the accuracy-locked structural truth (text identity, frame, inventory must survive every edit per svg_semantic_diff.py).
prior_art: Christensen, Marks, Shieber, 'An Empirical Study of Algorithms for Point-Feature Label Placement', ACM Transactions on Graphics 14(3):203-232, 1995 — the canonical PFLP formulation: NP-hard, four-position/eight-position discrete candidate model, slider model, and simulated annealing as the empirically best practical solver. https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/212332.212334 (open PDF: https://www.eecs.harvard.edu/shieber/Biblio/Papers/tog-final.pdf). This is the deterministic core for label-collision avoidance.

Bekos, Kaufmann, Symvonis, Wolff, 'Boundary Labeling: Models and Efficient Algorithms for Rectangular Maps', Computational Geometry 36(3):215-236, 2006 — leader-line theory for technical illustrations and medical atlases: type-s (straight), type-po (one-bend, two axis-parallel segments), type-opo (two-bend) leaders, non-crossing constraint, total-leader-length minimization. https://www1.pub.informatik.uni-wuerzburg.de/pub/wolff/pub/bksw-blmea-06.pdf

Cartographic naming-rule heritage (Imhof's positioning priorities) operationalized as the PFLP candidate-preference ordering used by Christensen-Marks-Shieber and the Map Library survey of placement algorithms. https://www.maplibrary.org/1398/label-placement-algorithms-for-automated-mapping/

Nature 'Formatting guide' figure typography conventions: sans-serif (Helvetica/Arial), panel letters 8pt bold upright, body text 5-7pt, single space between number and unit, SI nomenclature, avoid reversed/over-shaded type. https://www.nature.com/nature/for-authors/formatting-guide — the deterministic legibility/units rule set.

W3C SVG 1.1 Text module (text-anchor, dominant-baseline, tspan, textPath, startOffset) — the native SVG primitives the Hand emits for alignment, baseline control, and curved/leader-following labels. https://www.w3.org/TR/2003/REC-SVG11-20030114/text.html and https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Attribute/dominant-baseline
verified_libraries: D3-Labeler (tinker10/D3-Labeler, MIT) — D3 plug-in implementing Christensen-Marks-Shieber simulated annealing for label placement; declares label+anchor positions, bounds, and nsweeps (default 1000). Reference implementation of the deterministic placement solver. https://github.com/tinker10/D3-Labeler

label-locator (marcusand/label-locator) — TypeScript port of D3-Labeler's simulated annealing that is renderer-agnostic and does NOT mutate its input; ideal model for a pure label-position solver feeding the Hand's SVG emitter rather than a chart library. https://github.com/marcusand/label-locator

d3fc-label-layout (ColinEberhardt/d3fc-label-layout) — D3 layout offering greedy, simulated-annealing, and remove-overlap strategies; shows the strategy-pluggable contract (cheap greedy default, SA when needed). https://github.com/ColinEberhardt/d3fc-label-layout

fonttools (Python, MIT) — reads TrueType/OpenType to get per-glyph xMin/xMax/yMin/yMax and advance width WITHOUT rendering; the deterministic text-bbox engine that replaces the dead pdftotext -bbox path with native font metrics. https://pypi.org/project/fonttools/ and https://fonttools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ttLib/ttFont.html

MathJax 4 SVG output processor — converts LaTeX/MathML to standalone SVG <path> glyphs (character paths reused via <use>, stroke-width in 1/1000 em); the verified engine for rendering math expressions and unit/subscript typography as accuracy-locked SVG paths. https://docs.mathjax.org/en/latest/output/svg.html

freetype-py / Pillow ImageFont as a cross-check renderer for measured advance widths and kerning when fonttools metrics need a rasterized sanity check (font metrics depth: OS/2 sTypoAscender/sTypoDescender). https://github.com/the-sett/svg-text-fonts (text-to-SVG-path + bbox prior art)
deterministic_ops: measure_text_bbox(text, font, size_pt) -> {x,y,w,h,baseline,advance} via fonttools glyph metrics (no rendering); the geometric primitive every other op consumes. Replaces the dead pdftotext -bbox I/O from check_label_path_proximity.py / check_text_boundary_clash.py with native-SVG font metrics.

place_label(anchor, candidate_positions) -> chosen position: discrete 4-position then 8-position PFLP candidate model (Christensen-Marks-Shieber), cost = #overlaps + cartographic position-preference penalty; greedy first (d3fc style), escalate to simulated_annealing_layout(nsweeps) only when greedy leaves residual overlaps. Fully reproducible given a fixed RNG seed logged in the manifest.

route_leader(anchor, label_box, obstacles) -> SVG path: emit type-s / type-po / type-opo leader (Bekos et al.) choosing the lowest-bend non-crossing route with minimal total length; deterministic given obstacle set. Connects detached labels (fixes label_target_detached / floating_semantic_cue).
set_text_anchor / set_dominant_baseline(element, value): mechanical alignment — snap labels to start/middle/end and alphabetic/central/hanging baselines per the alignment intent; reproducible.

align_to_grid(labels, axis, tolerance_pt): snap a set of labels to a shared baseline/left-edge so columns and panel letters line up (kills uniform_line_weight_monotony's typographic cousin — ragged annotation).
render_math(latex) -> svg_paths via MathJax SVG: deterministic LaTeX->path for formulas, sub/superscripts, vectors; output is accuracy-locked geometry, not editable text.
format_units(value, unit): enforce single space number<->unit, SI nomenclature, italic variable / roman unit, thousands separator (Nature rule); pure string+style transform.
enforce_min_print_size(element, min_pt=5, target_pt=7): clamp/flag font sizes below the journal floor at print scale (drives print_scale_unreadable). Deterministic from declared output width.

detect_label_collisions(labels, paths, panels) -> findings: native-SVG port of check_collisions.py + check_label_path_proximity.py + check_text_boundary_clash.py — IoU/segment-rect-distance logic reused verbatim, fed by measure_text_bbox instead of PDF words; emits the MICRO_DEFECT_KINDS (line_crosses_label, label_path_near_miss, label_crosses_panel_boundary, label_overflows_row_box, label_backdrop_overflows_outline, etc.).

add_label_halo(element, color, width): mechanical legibility backdrop (text outline/halo) when a label must sit over shaded geometry (Nature 'avoid reversed type over shading' workaround); bounded so backdrop never overflows the panel outline.
taste_ops: choose_label_strategy(panel): decide inline-label vs. leader-callout vs. boundary-label vs. legend-key — a compositional judgement (gap D) that the deterministic router only executes once chosen; needs a critique signal.

balance_annotation_density(panel): decide which labels to keep, merge, demote to legend, or drop so annotation does not compete with the science (antipattern annotation_noise_competes_with_science); taste call on what is essential.

set_label_hierarchy(labels): assign type weight/size/case to express hero vs. supporting vs. incidental labels (gap D visual_hierarchy / weak_hero_anchor; low_authority_typography); the solver can render any hierarchy but cannot decide it.

tune_leader_expressiveness(leader): choose straight vs. gentle organic-curve leader and its hand-drawn weight modulation (gap B childish_shape_language / gap A handcrafted_finish) — aesthetic, drawn on the freedom layer on top of structural truth.

resolve_residual_overlap_by_artdirection(labels): when PFLP/SA cannot place all labels without overlap (NP-hard, no feasible layout), the taste op decides the human-style compromise (reposition the drawing, shrink a region, accept a callout) — routes to needs_human_art_direction.

pick_font_pairing_and_case(figure): enforce one coherent sans-serif voice and small-caps/case decisions across panels for cross_panel_grammar — a paper-wide taste call seeded by the style-token system (piece #4).
proposed_agent_api: hand.measure_text(text, font_token, size_pt) -> TextBox  # fonttools-backed, no render
hand.place_labels(anchors, label_specs, frame, obstacles, strategy='greedy|annealing', seed) -> [Placement]  # Christensen-Marks-Shieber PFLP; seed logged to manifest for reproducibility
hand.route_leader(anchor, label_box, obstacles, kind='s|po|opo') -> svg_path  # Bekos boundary-labeling leader, non-crossing, min-length
hand.set_text_anchor(selector, 'start|middle|end') and hand.set_baseline(selector, 'alphabetic|central|hanging')  # alignment, on accuracy-locked nodes
hand.align_labels(selectors, axis='x|y', edge='baseline|left|center', tol_pt)  # grid snap
hand.render_math(latex, font_token) -> svg_group  # MathJax SVG paths, accuracy-locked
hand.format_units(value, unit, style_token) -> tspans  # SI spacing, italic-var/roman-unit, thousands sep
hand.add_label(text|math, target, placement='auto'|Placement, leader=None, layer='structural'|'aesthetic')  # high-level; layer flag selects bounded vs. free authoring
hand.add_text_halo(selector, color_token, width_pt)  # legibility backdrop, bounded to panel outline

hand.audit_labels(svg) -> [MicroDefect]  # native-SVG port of collision/proximity/boundary checks; emits MICRO_DEFECT_KINDS, every emitted edit re-runs svg_semantic_diff before write so text_identity_loss/frame_change BLOCKERs cannot pass

These are mechanical: a finding deterministically selects the op.", "Taste ops are DRIVEN BY the aesthetic vocabulary (QUALITY_AXIS label_annotation_semantics + composition_layout; AESTHETIC_ANTIPATTERN_IDS annotation_noise_competes_with_science -> balance_annotation_density; low_authority_typography + weak_hero_anchor -> set_label_hierarchy; childish_shape_language + dead_flat_vector_finish + handcrafted_finish gate -> tune_leader_expressiveness; AESTHETIC_LEVER_DIMENSIONS typography_authority / cross_panel_grammar -> pick_font_pairing_and_case). Verdicts {pass,weak,fail,needs_human}; route via SVG_DELTA_ROUTES {continue_svg_polish, accept_svg_polish, semantic_backport_required, needs_human_art_direction}. SVG_DELTA_REGRESSION_CATEGORIES.label_readability is the regression tripwire that reverts a taste edit that hurt legibility."]
amateur_failure_mode: ["Treating label placement as 'put text at the anchor coordinate' — skipping the PFLP candidate search, so labels overlap each other, the geometry, and panel rules. The pro move is the four/eight-position + slider candidate model with a collision-cost solver (Christensen-Marks-Shieber); the amateur emits one position and hopes.", "Drawing leaders as naive straight lines from label to target that cross other leaders and the drawing — instead of the non-crossing, bend-minimizing, length-minimizing boundary-labeling leaders (type-s/po/opo). Crossing leaders are the #1 tell of an auto-generated callout figure.", "Letting the LLM eyeball text width instead of measuring real font metrics — labels then overflow boxes, collide after export, and break at the wrong print scale. fonttools advance-width/bbox measurement (or MathJax for math) is non-negotiable; visual estimation is the amateur failure.", "Typesetting units and variables wrong — no space before the unit, italic units, roman variables, ASCII 'u' for micro, 'x10^3' instead of proper superscripts — violating SI/Nature conventions and instantly reading as non-publication-grade.
