The amateur mutates truth to satisfy aesthetics; the Hand only ever moves/leaders/halos labels on the freedom layer and re-runs svg_semantic_diff so text identity survives."]

focus: Compositional eye for the Illustrator's Hand (gap D ONLY): focal hierarchy, whitespace/breathing, alignment grids, balance/equilibrium, emphasis/de-emphasis WITHOUT distorting facts, and reading flow. Scope is piece #3 the Hand — HOW an agent manipulates SVG to compose like an illustrator — layered as DETERMINISTIC geometry ops (mechanical, reproducible: measure balance, snap to grid, solve alignment, quantify whitespace) vs TASTE ops (which node is the hero, how strong the emphasis, is the reading order right — each gated by a named critique signal). Every op routes through svg_semantic_diff (the immutable structural truth-lock) before write: composition may only re-position/re-weight/re-emphasize the EXISTING accuracy-locked node inventory and add decoration on a separate aesthetic layer; it may NEVER move a node so far it breaks a declared spatial relationship, drop/merge a node, or change text identity, frame, or color-by-id. Two-layer split is load-bearing: structural nodes keep tight bounds (alignment snaps and emphasis re-weights are bounded + relationship-preserving); a NEW aesthetic-freedom layer (background fields, whitespace shaping, focal vignette/halo, group-bracket strokes) is drawn ON TOP of truth with no inventory footprint.
prior_art: GRIDS (Dayama, Todi, Saarelainen, Oulasvirta, CHI 2020) — layout as Mixed-Integer Linear Programming: encodes alignment-between-elements, overall rectangularity, packing/whitespace, grouping, and preferential positioning as solvable constraints; the named objectives map 1:1 onto the Hand's deterministic alignment/grid ops, and its 'designer picks among diverse solutions / weights objectives' split is exactly the deterministic-vs-taste boundary. https://userinterfaces.aalto.fi/grids/ , https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.02921

Ngo et al. computational aesthetic metrics — 13–14 closed-form layout indices (balance, equilibrium, symmetry, density, proportion, unity, sequence). Balance = signed difference of element weight on each side of an axis; equilibrium = distance from element center-of-mass to physical center; density = fraction of non-informative whitespace. These are deterministic, reproducible scalars the Hand computes to drive composition. https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1071581909001220 , https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7085848/

Itti–Koch–Niebur bottom-up saliency + Graph-Based Visual Saliency (Harel, Koch, Perona, NeurIPS 2006) — render the SVG to raster, compute a saliency/importance map, and check whether the predicted focal point coincides with the intended scientific hero. This is the TASTE detector for focal hierarchy (weak_hero_anchor). https://github.com/shreelock/gbvs , http://papers.neurips.cc/paper/3095-graph-based-visual-saliency.pdf

Deep importance/attention maps for design (UMSI / DeepGaze II / MSI-Net; 'Predicting visual attention in graphic design documents') — element-level importance maps over rendered design; stronger hero/reading-order predictor than Itti-Koch for figure-like compositions, usable as the saliency critique backend. https://arxiv.org/html/2407.02439v1 , https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.1045

Rule of thirds + Gestalt grouping (proximity, similarity, common-region, continuity) — the design vocabulary for where the hero sits (third-intersections) and how the Hand's group-bracket / common-region ops legibly bind related nodes without moving them. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rule_of_thirds , https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2014/03/design-principles-visual-perception-and-the-principles-of-gestalt/

Cassowary linear constraint solver (Badros, Borning, Stuckey) — incremental simplex over linear equalities/inequalities with required-vs-preferred constraints; the canonical engine for 'align these edges, keep these gaps equal, prefer this position' that the Hand uses to snap structural nodes to an alignment grid deterministically. https://constraints.cs.washington.edu/cassowary/
verified_libraries: kiwisolver (PyPI, C++ Cassowary impl with Python bindings; latest release 2026-03-09, requires Python>=3.10; the constraint engine inside matplotlib's constrained_layout) — solves the Hand's alignment/equal-gap/preferred-position constraints as required+strength-weighted linear constraints, 10x-500x faster than original Cassowary. https://pypi.org/project/kiwisolver/ , https://kiwisolver.readthedocs.io/

svgpathtools (PyPI, mathandy/svgpathtools) — reads SVG paths, computes per-path/segment bounding boxes natively from Bezier geometry, plus tangents/normals/curvature; the native-SVG replacement for the dead pdftotext -bbox path used by check_collisions.py. Path segment classes Line/QuadraticBezier/CubicBezier/Arc. https://pypi.org/project/svgpathtools/ , https://github.com/mathandy/svgpathtools

svgelements (PyPI) — full SVG parsing with transform/matrix resolution and geometry/bbox; resolves nested group transforms to absolute coordinates so balance/whitespace metrics are computed in the real rendered frame, not local node space. https://pypi.org/project/svgelements/

shapely 2.x (PyPI/readthedocs) — polygon/box intersection, union, overlaps, area; deterministic backend for whitespace quantification (free-area polygons), bbox IoU overlap (reusing check_collisions' IoU logic native-SVG), and group convex-hull / common-region computation. https://shapely.readthedocs.io/en/stable/manual.html

GBVS reference implementation (shreelock/gbvs) and pySaliencyMap (akisatok, Itti-Koch-Niebur) — runnable saliency backends to render-and-score the composition's predicted focal point for the hero-anchor critique signal. https://github.com/shreelock/gbvs , (Itti-Koch Python impl) https://github.com/akisatok/pySaliencyMap

drawsvg / svgwrite (PyPI) — programmatic SVG authoring incl. cubic & smooth-cubic Bezier path emission; how the Hand writes the NEW aesthetic-freedom layer (background fields, focal halo, group brackets) as real path/gradient nodes without touching the locked inventory. https://pypi.org/project/drawsvg/ , https://svgwrite.readthedocs.io/en/latest/classes/path.html
deterministic_ops: measure_composition(svg) -> metrics: compute Ngo-style scalars over the resolved-transform bbox inventory (svgelements) — balance_x/balance_y (signed weighted-area difference across each axis), equilibrium (center-of-mass offset from frame center, normalized), density (1 - informative_area/frame_area via shapely free-area), proportion, symmetry. Pure, reproducible, no judgment; emits the numbers TASTE ops and critique consume.

build_alignment_grid(svg, tolerance) -> grid: cluster existing node edges/centers (left/right/top/bottom/cx/cy) within tolerance into candidate gridlines; GRIDS-style rectangular dissection over the current inventory. Detection only — proposes lines, moves nothing.

snap_to_grid(node_ids, grid, max_delta) -> bounded translate ops: solve edge-alignment + equal-gap as kiwisolver REQUIRED constraints with each node's original position as a strong-but-weak preferred constraint, emit per-node translate deltas; HARD-bounded (structural nodes keep a small ceiling, default 10px, lifted only where a declared relationship is provably preserved). Reuses the existing executor's translate action + bound check.

measure_whitespace(svg) -> free_area_polygons + gutter_report: shapely-subtract all node bboxes from frame, report free-region polygons, min inter-node gutter, and cramped/dead-zone flags (Ngo density + GRIDS packing). Deterministic.
equalize_gutters(node_ids, target_gap, max_delta) -> bounded translate ops: kiwisolver equal-gap constraints to regularize spacing within a row/column group; bounded, relationship-preserving.

compute_saliency_map(rendered_png) -> saliency_raster + predicted_focus_xy: render SVG to raster, run GBVS/Itti-Koch, return the saliency peak coordinates. Deterministic given a fixed model+seed; the measurement half of the hero-anchor check (the JUDGMENT of whether that focus is correct is TASTE).
rule_of_thirds_targets(frame) -> 4 intersection points + 2 thirds-lines: pure geometry; the candidate anchor positions emphasis/hero ops snap toward.

bbox_inventory_native(svg) -> per-node absolute bbox: svgpathtools+svgelements bbox of every locked node in resolved frame coords; the native-SVG replacement for check_collisions.py's pdftotext bbox, feeding every metric above and the collision/proximity checks reused from the TikZ era.
taste_ops: designate_hero(svg, intent) -> hero_node_id: choose WHICH scientific element is the focal anchor. Pure judgment — depends on the message storyline, not geometry. Driven by critique axis message_storyline + weak_hero_anchor; the agent proposes, critique confirms the chosen hero matches scientific intent. Output then feeds deterministic emphasis ops.

set_emphasis(hero_id, strength) -> bounded re-weight ops on structural layer + halo op on aesthetic layer: HOW MUCH to emphasize — choose among bounded stroke-weight increase (reuse set_stroke_width within [0.5,2.0] ratio), de-emphasis of competitors via set_opacity/set_fill_opacity (reuse existing actions), size nudge within bound, and a NEW aesthetic-layer focal vignette/halo. Taste = the strength dial and the emphasis/de-emphasis balance; gated by visual_hierarchy + line_weight_rhythm (avoid uniform_line_weight_monotony) and overdecorated_or_cartoonish (don't over-halo).

choose_reading_flow(svg, intent) -> ordered node path + drawing-order fixes: decide the intended Z/F reading order of panels/components and whether current saliency scanpath matches it; may reorder SVG draw order (paint order only, inventory-preserving) and nudge leader/flow so the eye travels setup->mechanism->result. Taste = what the correct narrative order IS; gated by panel_role_coherence + reading-flow, cross-checked against the deterministic saliency scanpath.

balance_composition(metrics, intent) -> targeted bounded nudges OR aesthetic-layer counterweight: given measure_composition's balance/equilibrium numbers, DECIDE whether the imbalance is intentional (a deliberately heavy hero) or a defect, and whether to fix it by nudging a structural node (bounded) or by adding an aesthetic-layer counterweight (label block, background field) rather than moving facts. Taste = is this imbalance expressive or sloppy; gated by composition_layout + cramped_or_dead_whitespace.

shape_whitespace(free_polygons, intent) -> aesthetic-layer breathing ops: decide where to ADD breathing room vs where density is doing semantic work (a dense apparatus cluster is meaningful). Acts via aesthetic-layer background fields / margin framing and group spacing, never by deleting nodes. Taste = which whitespace is 'dead' vs 'restful'; gated by whitespace_breathing + cramped_or_dead_whitespace + maturity_restraint.

bind_group(node_ids, intent) -> aesthetic-layer common-region/bracket op: Gestalt grouping — draw a NEW common-region panel, proximity bracket, or subtle tint band on the aesthetic layer to legibly bind related nodes the eye should read together. Taste = which nodes form a perceptual group and whether a bracket helps or adds annotation_noise; gated by subregion_integration + cross_panel_grammar + annotation_noise_competes_with_science.
proposed_agent_api: hand.measure(fixture) -> CompositionReport{ balance_x, balance_y, equilibrium, density, free_polygons, gutter_min, grid_candidates, saliency_focus_xy, hero_match: bool|null, thirds_targets } — single read-only deterministic call the agent runs FIRST to see the composition numerically; pure, no writes.

hand.plan(fixture, ops:[CompositionOp]) -> Plan — CompositionOp reuses the existing recipe shape {id, layer:'structural'|'aesthetic', selector:{element_id|css_class|text_exact}, action, rationale, semantic_guard{allowed,reason}, critique_signal} and EXTENDS the action set with: snap_to_grid, equalize_gutters, set_emphasis, deemphasize, reorder_paint, add_aesthetic_layer{halo|vignette|background_field|group_bracket|common_region|counterweight}. Returns dry-run deltas + per-op bound check + which layer each writes to. No writes (mirrors plan_svg_polish).

hand.apply(fixture, plan, --write) -> polished.svg + decision_log entry — executes only after (a) every structural-layer op passes its bound (translate ceiling, stroke ratio [0.5,2.0], opacity [0,1]) AND (b) the whole result passes svg_semantic_diff against the source (text identity, frame, element inventory, color-by-id invariant). Aesthetic-layer ops are written into a dedicated <g id='aesthetic-hand-layer'> so they add zero footprint to the locked inventory and the diff sees them as additive decoration, not structural drift. Reuses apply_svg_polish + the immutable-source/exports pattern.

hand.relationship_guard(fixture, ops) -> pass|violation — NEW structural invariant on top of svg_semantic_diff: from bbox_inventory_native, assert that declared spatial relationships (A-left-of-B, A-inside-B, A-connected-to-B-by-leader) survive every translate; a snap that would reorder or detach facts is rejected BEFORE the diff. This is the composition-specific half of piece #6 (accuracy lock) — geometry-relationship preservation, not just inventory preservation.

hand.decision_log(fixture) -> append-only ledger — reuses the manifest provenance pattern: records each composition op's rationale, the critique_signal that triggered it, before/after composition metrics, and editor∈{agent_assisted,human}. Reproducibility = version-controlled SVG + this log (NOT pixel-lock), per the decided philosophy.
critique_signal: ["Reuse the existing critique vocabulary verbatim as the trigger bus; each composition op subscribes to a named signal. Focal hierarchy: aesthetic antipattern weak_hero_anchor + axis visual_hierarchy fire designate_hero/set_emphasis; verified by deterministic saliency (compute_saliency_map focus vs designated hero) — if saliency peak != hero, emit weak_hero_anchor with the measured offset as evidence. Whitespace/breathing: cramped_or_dead_whitespace + lever whitespace_breathing fire shape_whitespace/equalize_gutters; evidence = measure_whitespace density + dead-zone polygons (deterministic). Balance: composition_layout axis + measure_composition's balance/equilibrium scalars; an out-of-band equilibrium drives balance_composition, but the TASTE op decides expressive-vs-defect. Alignment: a new micro-defect kind (extend MICRO_DEFECT_KINDS) edge_misalignment_off_grid from build_alignment_grid residuals drives snap_to_grid. Reading flow: panel_role_coherence + the deterministic saliency scanpath order vs intended order drives choose_reading_flow. Line-weight emphasis: uniform_line_weight_monotony + lever line_weight_rhythm bound set_emphasis. Guardrails that BLOCK over-composition: overdecorated_or_cartoonish + poster_gradient_decoration + annotation_noise_competes_with_science cap halo/vignette/bracket strength; maturity_restraint gates the whole aesthetic layer. Every write still routes through svg_semantic_diff finding kinds (text_identity_loss=BLOCKER, frame_change/group_transform_risk=MAJOR) which OVERRIDE any aesthetic gain — route semantic_backport_required wins over svg_polish."]
amateur_failure_mode: ["Moving facts to make it pretty: nudging a structural node onto a thirds-intersection or an alignment grid so far it breaks a declared spatial relationship (label now points at the wrong arrow, A no longer inside B, leader detaches). Mitigation: hand.relationship_guard rejects before svg_semantic_diff; structural translates stay bounded and relationship-preserving — composition re-weights and decorates, it does not relocate truth.", "Hero by raw size/contrast instead of scientific importance: cranking saliency on whatever is geometrically biggest, making a decorative element out-shout the actual mechanism. Mitigation: designate_hero is TASTE bound to message_storyline; saliency is only the verification that the CHOSEN hero wins, never the chooser.", "Decoration masquerading as composition: poster gradients, drop-shadow halos, heavy group boxes (poster_gradient_decoration, childish_shape_language, generic_template_look) added on the aesthetic layer until the figure looks like a slide, not a journal figure. Mitigation: overdecorated_or_cartoonish + maturity_restraint gates with hard strength caps on every aesthetic-layer op.", "Symmetry/balance fetish: forcing perfect equilibrium (balance==0) and killing every asymmetry, flattening a deliberately weighted hero composition into a dead-centered, monotonous grid (cramped_or_dead_whitespace, dead_flat_vector_finish). Mitigation: balance_composition treats imbalance as possibly expressive; the metric informs, the critique decides, and there is no auto-center.", "Over-alignment / over-gridding: snapping every node to a rigid grid until the figure reads as a generic template and loses the hand-touch organic quality that is the whole northstar. Mitigation: snap is tolerance-clustered and opt-in per node, bounded, and competes against the hand_craft / handcrafted_finish levers — alignment serves legibility, it is not applied globally by default.", "Treating composition as a one-shot solve: running an MILP/auto-layout over the whole figure and overwriting the author's intent. Mitigation: the Hand emits bounded per-node ops the agent and critique iterate on (plan -> diff -> critique -> apply), with the decision log as the reproducible record — never a black-box relayout."]

focus: Piece #3, the Illustrator's Hand: the mapping from a vision-critique signal to a concrete, agent-callable SVG edit op, plus the immutable guard that lets aesthetic freedom (gaps A=finish, B=organic curves, D=composition) ride ON TOP of structural/scientific truth without ever breaking it. Core design move = a TWO-LAYER SVG contract: (1) accuracy-locked structural nodes whose inventory (text identity, frame, element/color-by-id, path/marker counts, transform bounds) is byte-checkable-invariant via the ported svg_semantic_diff engine, and (2) a hand:* aesthetic-freedom overlay drawn strictly on top, where Bezier/gradient/organic authoring is unbounded but is still gated so it cannot occlude, detach, or displace a locked label/target. Every Hand op is classified DETERMINISTIC (mechanical, parametrized, reproducible from a recipe+seed -> no critique needed to EXECUTE) vs TASTE (needs a critique signal to decide whether/where/how-much, but once decided executes deterministically). The Hand widens the existing 5-action recipe contract {selector, action, rationale, semantic_guard} into a draw-op set, routing every op through svg_semantic_diff before write; tikz_patch/continue_tikz become dead enum members replaced by native-SVG draw ops. WYSIWYG-drag (gap C) is out of scope and has zero footprint since nothing in the pipeline is an editing UI.
prior_art: LayoutRectifier (Shen et al., Computer Graphics Forum 2025, arXiv:2508.11177): two-stage OPTIMIZATION post-processing that rectifies generated layouts with MINIMAL deviation -- stage 1 snaps elements to an exemplar GRID SYSTEM via discrete search (fixes misalignment), stage 2 a box-containment function removes overlaps/enforces containment. This is the exact template for the Hand's DETERMINISTIC composition-rectifier ops (snap_to_grid, resolve_overlap) and proves the 'nudge toward grid, minimize deviation' contract that bounds aesthetic edits.

Rough.js (roughjs.com; shihn.ca/posts/2020/roughjs-algorithms): hand-drawn/sketchy SVG primitives where every point gets a random offset; CRUCIALLY it accepts a SEED so the random sequence is reproducible across renders. This is the verified mechanism for gap-B organic/sketchy strokes that are still DETERMINISTIC+version-controllable (seed in the recipe = reproducible SVG, satisfying the 'reproducibility = versioned SVG + decision log, not pixel-lock' philosophy).

Adobe Illustrator Width Tool + Stroke Width Profiles + Appearance Panel (helpx.adobe.com; peachpit.com/articles/article.aspx?p=2979069): the professional model for NON-DESTRUCTIVE variable-width/tapered strokes -- a width profile is metadata layered ON TOP of an unchanged underlying path, exactly mapping to the Hand's overlay-layer split (aesthetic finish added without mutating the structural path's geometry).

Chaikin corner-cutting (Joy, cs.unc.edu Chaikins-Algorithm.pdf) + Catmull-Rom interpolation + Ramer-Douglas-Peucker simplification (smoothr/smoothr R pkg implements all three): canonical DETERMINISTIC curve operations -- RDP removes redundant vertices, Chaikin/Catmull-Rom smooth a polyline into organic Bezier curves with consistent results for identical inputs. These are the verified math primitives for the deterministic side of gap-B.

InstructPix2Pix (Brock/Holynski/Efros, arXiv:2211.09800) classifier-free-guidance structure preservation: the raster analogue of the problem -- it follows a written EDIT INSTRUCTION while a guidance weight preserves input STRUCTURE. Establishes the 'critique-text -> bounded edit that must not break structure' pattern; the Hand's SVG-native analogue replaces CFG's soft structure-preservation with a HARD inventory-invariance guard (svg_semantic_diff), which is stronger because it cannot hallucinate geometry.

NeuralSVG (arXiv:2501.03992) and semantic-layer recovery work (SemLayer arXiv:2603.24039; Vector Prism arXiv:2512.14336): confirm the research consensus that semantically-LAYERED vector representations (separating background/structure/decoration into independent primitives) enable local, semantically-meaningful manipulation that flattened paths cannot -- direct support for the structural-vs-aesthetic two-layer architecture.

CenterLine (aescripts.com/centerline): production tool using Zhang-Suen thinning + Schneider Bezier fitting + RDP to map a distance-transform width back onto vector centerlines -- a worked pipeline proving the deterministic chain from raster intent to organic variable-width vector strokes.
verified_libraries: svgpathtools (PyPI; mathandy/svgpathtools; latest release 2025-11-30, Python >=3.8): primary Bezier/path engine -- Line/QuadraticBezier/CubicBezier/Arc/Path classes, tangent/normal/curvature, Bezier<->poly1d, continuous-subpath splitting. The deterministic geometry core for all freeform/organic Hand ops.
lxml: SVG tree read/write/selector engine; the existing executor uses stdlib xml.etree.ElementTree but lxml adds real XPath/CSS selection needed to widen the selector contract. Actively maintained.
Rough.js (JS, <9kB, Canvas+SVG, seedable): the sketchy/hand-drawn renderer for gap-B; seed makes it reproducible. (Node-callable from the Hand; svg2roughjs exists to convert existing SVG.)
smoothr (R) / standalone Chaikin+RDP+Catmull-Rom implementations: deterministic polyline-smoothing and simplification; algorithms are tiny and portable to Python alongside svgpathtools.

svgutils (btel/svg_utils, depends on lxml, scientist-targeted figure assembly) and svgelements (parsing, last release 2023): usable for composition/placement but BOTH less active -- treat as reference, prefer lxml+svgpathtools as the maintained core. NOTE: svgwrite is explicitly UNMAINTAINED (last update 2022) -- do NOT adopt it.
drawsvg (PyPI, Python 3, renders SVG->PNG/MP4): viable for programmatic emit + raster preview to feed the host-session vision critique loop.
deterministic_ops: snap_to_grid(selector, grid): LayoutRectifier-style discrete search snapping an element's bbox edges to an inferred/declared grid. Reproducible; fixes cramped_or_dead_whitespace + alignment. Bounded like the existing 10px translate when applied to a structural node.

resolve_overlap(selectors): box-containment optimization (LayoutRectifier stage 2) that minimally displaces overlapping elements; on structural nodes only sanctioned-bound translates, on overlay layer free.

smooth_path(path_selector, method=chaikin|catmull_rom, iterations): deterministic polyline->organic-Bezier smoothing via svgpathtools. AESTHETIC-LAYER ONLY for structural paths (emits a hand:* sibling); never mutates a locked path's d in place.
simplify_path(path_selector, epsilon): RDP vertex reduction; deterministic given epsilon.

apply_width_profile(path_selector, profile, seed?): Illustrator-WidthTool-style variable-width stroke expansion (svgpathtools offset) emitted as a hand:* filled overlay sibling; original stroke untouched.
sketchify(selector, roughness, seed): Rough.js pass with a recorded seed -> reproducible hand-drawn overlay (gap B).
set_stroke_width / set_opacity / set_fill_opacity / set_stroke_opacity / translate (the 5 existing executor actions, ratio/px-bounded): retained verbatim for structural-node micro-correction.

harmonize_palette(selectors, token_set): remap fills to locked design-token palette (the SVG successor to the .sty Style Lock) -- deterministic given the token table; on structural-by-id nodes it must MATCH the locked color (semantic_color_remap guard), only free on decoration.
align_distribute(selectors, axis, mode): deterministic edge/center align + even-gap distribution.
rasterize_preview(selector_or_full, dpi): deterministic SVG->PNG (drawsvg/cairosvg) to feed the vision critique -- the bridge op, not a draw op.
taste_ops: choose_hero_anchor(): WHICH element becomes the visual focus (drives a downstream deterministic scale/weight bump). Pure judgment -> needs critique signal weak_hero_anchor / visual_hierarchy.

decide_line_weight_rhythm(): assign a weight HIERARCHY (which strokes are primary vs secondary vs hairline) -- the assignment is taste; once chosen, each set_stroke_width is deterministic. Driven by uniform_line_weight_monotony / line_weight_rhythm.

decide_whitespace_breathing(): judge WHERE the composition is cramped/dead and how much margin to open -> then deterministic snap_to_grid/translate. Driven by cramped_or_dead_whitespace / whitespace_breathing.

decide_organic_curvature(): judge WHICH connectors/membranes should read as organic vs mechanical, and the roughness/curvature amount -> then deterministic smooth_path/sketchify with chosen params. Driven by childish_shape_language (too-cartoonish) vs dead_flat_vector_finish. This is the gap-B taste gate.

decide_decoration_restraint(): judge whether a proposed hand:* flourish ADDS explanatory value or is noise -> accept/reject. Driven by decorative_detail_without_explanatory_value / overdecorated_or_cartoonish / annotation_noise_competes_with_science / maturity_restraint.
decide_color_harmony(): pick the palette mood/accent within the locked token set. Driven by color_harmony lever.
decide_cross_panel_grammar(): judge whether panels share a consistent visual language and choose the unifying move. Driven by cross_panel_grammar / panel_style_mismatch.
proposed_agent_api: draw_overlay(layer='hand:<purpose>', ops=[...]): append a NEW aesthetic-freedom group; geometry-bounds-EXEMPT but passes occlusion/detachment guard (must not cover a locked text bbox or sit between a leader line and its target). The single entry point for unbounded Bezier/gradient/organic authoring.

edit_structural(op): the existing bounded executor path -- only the 5 sanctioned actions, 10px/[0.5,2.0] bounds, must leave svg_semantic_diff inventory invariant (text/frame/id/color-by-id/path-marker counts unchanged). For micro-correcting truth nodes.
plan(recipe): dry-run returning per-op {layer, action, bounds-check, predicted_inventory_delta} WITHOUT writing -- reuses the recipe.plan pattern; now also reports which layer each op targets.

commit(recipe): apply, then run svg_semantic_diff(source, result); if any BLOCKER/MAJOR (text_identity_loss, frame_change, unsupported_svg_feature, group_transform_risk, out-of-bound marker_or_path_change on structural layer) -> REJECT the whole write (semantic_backport_required), never partial-write. Aesthetic-layer path/marker additions are whitelisted by being inside a hand:* group so they don't trip element_inventory_change against the locked set.

Recipe op shape (widened from the existing contract): {id, layer:'structure'|'hand:*', class (extended edit taxonomy), determinism:'deterministic'|'taste', selector:{element_id|css_class|text_exact|xpath}, action:{type, params, seed?}, driven_by:<critique signal id> (REQUIRED for determinism=taste), rationale, semantic_guard:{allowed, reason}}.

Extended edit taxonomy (8 existing + new): keep label_micro_position, leader_line_micro_position, stroke_polish, icon_detail, spacing_balance, color_opacity_polish, typography_cleanup, export_cleanup; ADD organic_curve_authoring, variable_width_stroke, grid_recompose, hero_emphasis, palette_harmonize, decorative_overlay, sketch_finish -- the last several are hand:*-layer-only.
manifest/decision-log entry per commit: {ops, seeds, driven_by signals, svg_semantic_diff verdict, input hashes} -> the version-controlled reproducibility ledger (replaces pixel-lock).
critique_signal: ["Each Hand op is DRIVEN BY an existing critique vocabulary token (no new signal language needed -- the reuse map's A/B/D gap mapping is the routing table). Concrete signal->op routes: visual_hierarchy/weak_hero_anchor (lever hero_hierarchy) -> choose_hero_anchor then deterministic scale+set_stroke_width emphasis. uniform_line_weight_monotony (lever line_weight_rhythm) -> decide_line_weight_rhythm then set_stroke_width set. cramped_or_dead_whitespace (lever whitespace_breathing) -> decide_whitespace_breathing then snap_to_grid/resolve_overlap (LayoutRectifier). dead_flat_vector_finish + handcrafted_finish gate slot (gap A) -> apply_width_profile + harmonize_palette gradients. childish_shape_language vs dead_flat (gap B) -> decide_organic_curvature then smooth_path/sketchify(seed). generic_template_look/panel_style_mismatch (cross_panel_grammar) -> decide_cross_panel_grammar. overdecorated_or_cartoonish/decorative_detail_without_explanatory_value/annotation_noise (maturity_restraint) -> decide_decoration_restraint (often REJECT). low_authority_typography -> typography_cleanup + hero type weight. Micro-defect kinds (line_crosses_label, wire_crosses_label, arrow_tip_fused, label_target_detached, label_path_near_miss) -> deterministic leader_line_micro_position/translate within bounds. The op's verdict/route reuses AESTHETIC_GATE_ROUTES with tikz_patch/continue_tikz REMOVED and replaced by the new svg-native draw classes; SVG_DELTA_EVALUATION_STATES (improved/regressed/...) closes the loop after rasterize_preview re-critique.", "The guard is the load-bearing inversion: a TASTE op may only PROPOSE; execution is deterministic and every write must survive svg_semantic_diff. Aesthetic freedom is structurally confined to the hand:* layer + the 5 bounded structural micro-actions, so the critique can be wrong about taste without ever being able to make the schematic scientifically wrong."]
amateur_failure_mode: ["Letting the Hand mutate a structural path's `d`/geometry directly to 'make it prettier' (in-place smooth/offset/sketchify on a locked node) -- this is how a real illustrator silently moves an arrow off its target or warps an apparatus into something scientifically false. The discipline: organic/finish ops ALWAYS emit a hand:* sibling overlay (Illustrator-Appearance-Panel non-destructive model); the truth path's `d` is immutable, enforced by svg_semantic_diff path/transform inventory.", "Treating taste ops as if they execute directly (LLM 'eyeballs' new coordinates) instead of: critique decides params -> a DETERMINISTIC, seeded, recipe-logged op executes -> re-critique. Direct freehand numeric edits are non-reproducible and unverifiable, breaking the versioned-SVG reproducibility contract.", "Carrying over the TikZ-era nudge ceilings (10px / [0.5,2.0]) onto the AESTHETIC layer, which strangles gaps A/B (an illustrator-grade flourish or tapered organic stroke needs far more than 10px) -- OR the opposite error, LIFTING the bounds on the STRUCTURAL layer, which lets a label drift off its target. The fix is the two-layer split: bounds live on structure, freedom lives on hand:*.", "Allowing the aesthetic overlay to OCCLUDE or detach truth (a gradient blob over a locked label, a decorative curve threaded between a leader line and its target) -- 'on top of truth' must mean visually-above-but-non-interfering, so the overlay still passes an occlusion/detachment guard (reusing label_backdrop_overflows_outline / label_target_detached / floating_semantic_cue micro-defect checks against the LOCKED bboxes).", "Adopting svgwrite (UNMAINTAINED since 2022) or hand-rolling Bezier math instead of svgpathtools (released 2025-11-30), and skipping seeds on Rough.js/jitter ops -- producing unreproducible, unversionable output that defeats the decision-log-not-pixel-lock reproducibility philosophy.", "Partial-writing a recipe: applying the ops that passed and leaving the schematic in a half-edited state when one op trips the guard. commit() must be all-or-nothing -- any BLOCKER/MAJOR rejects the entire write (semantic_backport_required), matching the existing executor's refuse-to-overwrite stance."]
